problem_statement: The current 10-day +3% target is too broad: after score-weight repair, holdout return lift is positive but success lift remains negative versus the same-day market baseline. The target contract should be reviewed before another model change.

experiment_candidates.hypothesis: A trade target that requires +3% to occur before a meaningful adverse move will be closer to usable stock selection than the current eventually-touches-+3% target.
experiment_candidates.why_now: Weighting passed development monthly stability, but holdout still loses on success lift. Continuing to tune weights would repeat the old loop.
feature_changes: No new feature source is added.
Only label definitions are reviewed in this step.
Use next trading day open as the buy price and future open/high/low/close only inside the label layer.
model_changes: No model training in this step.
Do not modify integrated model heads or score weighting.
Prepare a target contract that a later main model run can consume.
pass_criteria: The new target can be computed from the three approved CSVs without future leakage into features.
The new target has enough completed holdout samples for validation.
The new target filters out high adverse-move cases that the old target counted as success.
The label distribution is not concentrated in one stock, one month, or one industry.
rejection_criteria: The new target leaves too few positive samples for training.
The new target is almost identical to the old target.
The new target requires unavailable data or manual judgment.
The new target introduces another user-facing model branch instead of replacing the target contract.
risk_notes: The -3% adverse move threshold is a starting contract, not a calibrated stop-loss recommendation.
This step compares label quality only; it must not produce formal candidates.

experiment_candidates.hypothesis: A fixed 5-day or 10-day close return target may be cleaner than any-day +3% because it removes path ambiguity.
experiment_candidates.why_now: The current any-day target may reward noisy spikes that are not reliable trade outcomes.
feature_changes: No new feature source is added.
Use future close returns only in label construction.
model_changes: No model training in this step.
Use this only as a target-contract comparison.
pass_criteria: The fixed-horizon target has enough positive and negative samples.
The target is easier to evaluate than any-day +3%.
The target does not collapse into pure market direction.
rejection_criteria: The target is too noisy or too dependent on exact exit day.
The target removes the user's original short-swing intent.
risk_notes: Fixed exits are clean for modeling but may ignore useful early profit opportunities.

experiment_candidates.hypothesis: A relative target may better answer whether the model picked the right stocks on the same day.
experiment_candidates.why_now: The failed main model shows that absolute +3% can be dominated by market regime.
feature_changes: No new feature source is added.
Use same-day completed label groups for market-relative label construction only.
model_changes: No model training in this step.
Use this only as a target-contract comparison.
pass_criteria: The target directly measures whether selected stocks outperform same-day opportunity.
The target has enough samples for train, development, and holdout.
The target does not become a hidden market-state label.
rejection_criteria: The target stops answering the user's practical trade question.
The target is too dependent on the universe composition of a single day.
risk_notes: A relative target can improve ranking but may no longer mean the trade made money.
